title: sterculia coccinea var. hamiltonii
intro: Sterculia coccinea var. hamiltonii, also known as the Red Corkwood or Indian Cork tree, is a plant species belonging to the family Malvaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant is said to offer several health benefits, such as reducing fever, treating skin conditions, and alleviating digestive issues. Herbal preparations of Sterculia coccinea var. hamiltonii include infusions and decoctions, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, or ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
